text: Welcome to Cisco's second quarter fiscal year 2026 financial results conference call at the request of Cisco today's conference is being recorded.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: If you have any objections, you may disconnect. Now I would like to introduce Sammy Bodry head of investor relations.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Sorry, you may begin. Good afternoon everyone. This is Sammy Bodry Cisco head of investor relations. I'm joined by Chuck Robbins, our chair and CEO and Mark Patterson, our CFO.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Cisco's earnings press release and supplemental information, including gap to non gap reconciliation are available on our investor relations website.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Following this call, we'll also make the recorded webcast and slides available on the website.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Throughout today's call, we'll be referencing both gap and non gap financial results. We will discuss product results in terms of revenue and geographic and customer results in terms of product orders, unless stated otherwise.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: All comparisons will be made on a year of a year basis. Please note that our discussion today will include forward looking statements, including our guidance for the third quarter and fiscal year 2026.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: These statements are subject to risks and uncertainties detailed on our SEC filings, particularly on most recent 10K and 10K reports, which identify important risk factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in our forward looking statements.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: With respect to guidance, please also see the slides and press release that accompany this call for further details. Cisco will not comment on its financial guidance during the quarter unless it is done through an explicit public disclosure. Now I'll turn it over to Chuck.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thanks, Sammy. And thank you all for joining us today. Q2 was a very strong quarter with revenue and earnings per share, both growing double digits and coming in above the high end of our guidance ranges.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We delivered record revenue on Q2, putting Cisco on track to deliver our strongest year yet as indicated in our guidance for the full year.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: In Q2, total revenue growth accelerated to 10% year over year with product revenue up 14%, driven by robust demand for AI infrastructure and campus networking solutions.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Our strong top line performance, combined with operating efficiencies and solid execution by our teams, contributed to non gap EPS growth of 11%, which continued to grow faster than revenue.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: This strong performance allowed us to return 3 billion in capital to shareholders in the quarter, bringing the total value return year to date to 6.6 billion.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Today, we also announced an increase to Cisco's dividend demonstrating our commitment to returning value to shareholders through consistent capital returns.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Our innovation engine is firing on all cylinders and our commitment to customers has never been stronger.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Last week, Cisco hosted its AI Summit, gathering AI visionaries and geopolitical experts to explore the economic, societal and business impacts of AI.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: While it was clear that expectations for adoption and execution are high, one major challenge still exists.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Legacy infrastructure was not designed for the performance, speed and security needs of AI.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Our strong first half of FY26 demonstrates both the power of our portfolio and the fundamental role we play in this once in a generation transition.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: With our industry leading networking portfolio powered by Silicon 1, AI native security solutions and operating systems, Cisco is well positioned to provide the critical infrastructure needed for the AI era.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: I'd also like to address the recent significant increases in memory prices across the market.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Leveraging our industry leading supply chain team, we are proactively implementing three key strategies.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: First, we have already announced price increases and will continue to monitor market trends and make additional adjustments as necessary.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Second, we are revising contractual terms with channel partners and customers to address evolving component prices.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Third, Cisco's operating scale and industry leading position help us negotiate favorable terms and secure supply to fulfill current and future demand.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Overall, we feel confident in our ability to manage this industry-wide dynamic better than our peers.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Now, let me comment on the strong demand we saw in Q2.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Overall, total product orders grew 18% year over year, even on top of double-digit growth in Q2 fiscal year 25.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Excluding hyperscalers, product orders were up 10% year over year, demonstrating the broad base demand we see for our technology globally.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Enterprise product orders were up 8% year over year in Q2 with strength across our entire networking portfolio.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Public sector orders were up 11% year over year with double-digit growth across all geographies.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Product orders from service provider and cloud customers accelerated in Q2, growing 65%, driven by triple-digit order growth across hyperscalers.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We also saw continued growth from telco and cable customers in Q2 with orders up almost 20% on a combined basis.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Now, some color on demand from a product perspective.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Growth in networking product orders continued to accelerate, reaching more than 20% in Q2 and marking the sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: driven by service provider routing, data center switching, campus switching, wireless, servers, and industrial IoT products.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Within our campus networking portfolio, we are seeing strong demand for our next generation switching, routing, and wireless products,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: which continue to ramp faster than prior product launches.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We are delivering AI-native capabilities across these products, including weaving security into the fabric of the network
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: and modernizing the operational stack of campus networks.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: These new capabilities, combined with an install base representing tens of billions of dollars across early catalyst generations nearing end of support,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: underpin the multi-year, multi-billion dollar refresh opportunity for Cisco.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We continue to see strong demand for our industrial IoT portfolio, which has now grown double digits for seven consecutive quarters.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: This demand is driven by onshoring of manufacturing to the United States, the increase of AI workloads at the network edge, and the emergence of physical AI.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: AI infrastructure orders taken from hyperscalers total 2.1 billion in Q2, compared to 1.3 billion just last quarter,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: and equal to the total orders taken in all of fiscal year 25, marking another significant acceleration in growth across our silicon systems and optics.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We shipped our 1 millionth silicon 1 chip in Q2, and planned to deploy our silicon 1 architecture across our high performance networking systems by fiscal year 29.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Just this week at Cisco Live Amsterdam, we introduced our 102.4 terabit per second G300 chip,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: positioning Cisco in an exclusive group of silicon providers delivering over 100 terabits per second switching speeds.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: In addition, we launched four new systems powered by G300.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: The Cisco 8000 and Nexus 9K 102.4 terabit systems offer flexible air-cooled options for traditional data center architectures,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: as well as liquid cooled option designed for the latest ground up facilities.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Silicon 1's programmability puts Cisco's silicon in a class of its own,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: capable of adapting to a wide range of use cases and network infrastructure designs.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We also announced two new plug-able optics, a 1.6 terabit per second OSFP,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: and an 800 gig LPO, both built with Cisco Silicon Photonics technology,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: delivering greater efficiency and reliability in high performance AI infrastructure.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: A case you reported as strong as quarter to date with triple digit growth in bookings.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: All major hyperscalers are deploying its market leading coherent plug-able optics for data center interconnect and scale across use cases.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We see growth in both 400 gig and 800 gig coherent optics and transponder shipments with 800 gig plug-ables ramping significantly.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Given the strong demand for our silicon 1 systems and optics,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: we now expect to take AI orders in excess of $5 billion and to recognize over $3 billion in AI infrastructure revenue from hyperscalers in FY26.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Beyond hyperscalers, we have a separate AI opportunity across Neocloud, Sovereign, and Enterprise customers.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We took $350 million in AI orders from these customers in Q2 and have a growing pipeline in excess of $2.5 billion for our high performance AI infrastructure portfolio.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We continue to develop our strategic partnerships to capture this opportunity.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: In Q2, we announced plans to form a joint venture with AMD inhumane to deliver up to 1 gigawatt of AI infrastructure by 2030.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: This joint venture expects to begin operations this calendar year with a plan to build out 100 megawatts in Saudi Arabia as phase one of the project.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We are seeing strong interest from European customers in our Sovereign Critical Infrastructure portfolio designed to operate in air-gapped on-prem environments,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: giving organizations control over sensitive data and critical infrastructure.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: As AI adoption accelerates, concerns over privacy, data governance, and regulatory compliance are top of mind for our customers,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: making sovereign solutions, and essential foundation for building digital trust.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Now, shifting to security.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: In Q2, we continue to see order growth across our new and refreshed products,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: which represent roughly one-third of our security portfolio, and include secure access, XDR, HyperShield, AI Defense, and refreshed firewalls.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Excluding the refreshed firewalls, over 1,000 new customers purchased these products in Q2,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: representing more than 100% growth quarter over quarter, and bringing the total of net new customers since launch to roughly 4,000.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We have also seen three consecutive quarters of double-digit growth in a number of firewalls ordered.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: For secure access specifically, we booked over 2.5 million users in Q2, and more than 50% of added customers were new logos.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: As the adoption of AI tools grow and agent AI increases at the network edge, we expect to see continued momentum in our SACI business,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: including secure access and SD-WAN.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: As mentioned in prior quarters, growth in our new and refreshed portfolio continues to be offset by a decline in our prior generation portfolio.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Turning to Splunk, we saw a similar trend in Q2 as seen in Q1, with continued acceleration to cloud subscriptions and fewer on-premise deals.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: While this shift is creating a drag-on revenue growth, which we expect to continue in the second half of fiscal year 26,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: cloud subscriptions enable greater adoption, expansion, and faster delivery of innovation to customers.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: So overall, we are pleased with this transition.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Splunk also continued to win new customers in Q2, reaching 500 new logos for the first half of fiscal year 26,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: and is on track to add 1,000 new logos for the year.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We are accelerating our innovation across our offerings, both for and with AI.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: At Cisco Live Amsterdam this week, we unveiled major AI defense and SACI advancements to help secure organizations as AI agents enter the workforce.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: AI defense can now scan models and repositories for vulnerabilities and provide an AI bill of materials for centralized governance.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: In Cisco SACI, we launched a new semantic inspection engine that can evaluate the intent of agentic interactions and block sophisticated context dependent threats.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We are also making agentic ops the operating model for AI-driven IT to enable autonomous troubleshooting, continuous optimization, and trusted validation.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We are deploying AI agents to work hand in hand with human administrators within our product dashboards, AI assistants, and AI Canvas.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We continue to make AI advancements internally with expanded use cases in Q2 across nearly every organization.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Today, the majority of our product developers are using AI coding assistance and working alongside agents which help us innovate faster across our portfolio.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Currently, over 90% of customer experience support cases are touched by AI and automation, enabling us to resolve a greater proportion of complex cases within one day,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: and contributing to our highest ever customer satisfaction scores.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Additional use cases across sales, security and trust, supply chain, and corporate functions are also providing significant cost savings and efficiency gains.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: To summarize, we see strong demand for our solutions across all customer markets and geographies solidifying Cisco's role in providing the critical infrastructure needed for this once in a generation transition.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: The value of our innovation is exemplified by Silicon One, which positions Cisco for the broadest range of AI deployments, even the most technologically challenging.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: And with over 40 years of customer trust and global scale, Cisco is committed to leading in the AI era to drive breakthrough innovation, manage complexity and risk, and deliver faster business outcomes to customers globally.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Now I'll turn it over to Mark for more detail on the quarter and our outlook.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Thanks, Chuck.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We delivered another strong quarter with record revenue in Q2 and both operating margin and earnings per share coming in above the high end of our guidance.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: For the quarter, total revenue was 15.3 billion, up 10% year over year.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Non gap net income was 4.1 billion, up 10%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: And non gap earnings per share was $1.04, up 11%, demonstrating continuing operating leverage with non gap earnings per share growing faster than revenue.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Looking at our Q2 revenue in more detail, total product revenue was 11.6 billion, up 14% and services revenue was 3.7 billion down 1% year over year.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Networking was again the standout with growth of 21%, driven by AI infrastructure and campus refresh.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We saw double digit growth and campus switching, data center switching, wireless, service provider routing, enterprise routing and compute.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Security was down 4%, reflecting similar dynamics discussed last quarter with declines in prior generation products in the transition in our Splunk business from an on-prem deal to cloud subscriptions.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Partially offset by growth and new and refresh products.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Collaboration posted solid growth of 6%, led by double digit growth and devices, as well as growth in CPAS, WebEx and cloud contact center.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Looking at our recurring metrics, total RPO was 43.4 billion, up 5%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Product RPO grew 8% of which the long term portion was $11.8 billion, up 11%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Total ARR ended the quarter at 31 billion, an increase of 3% with product ARR growth of 6%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: The subscription revenue, total subscription revenue was 7.8 billion and represented 51% of Cisco's total revenue.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Total software revenue was 5.7 billion, up 2%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Q2 product orders were up 18% year over year.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Product orders were up double digits across all geographic segments with the Americas up 23%,
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Amia up 11%, and APJC up 15%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Product orders were also up across all customer markets with service provider and cloud up 65%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Public sector up 11% and enterprise up 8%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Total non-GAP, gross margin, came in at 67.5% down 120 basis points year over year.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Non-GAP product gross margin was 66.4% down 130 basis points, primarily driven by negative impacts from mix and higher memory costs, partially offset by productivity improvements.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Non-GAP services gross margin was 70.9% down 70 basis points.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We continue our focus on enhancing profitability and driving financial discipline with non-GAP operating margin at 34.6% above the high end of our guidance range.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Our non-GAP tax rate was 19% for the quarter, shifting to the balance sheet.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We ended Q2 with total cash, cash equivalents and investments of 15.8 billion.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Operating cash flow was 1.8 billion down 19% due to the final transition tax payment of $2.3 billion from the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act and continued investments to meet growing overall demand as well as specifically for AI infrastructure.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: From a capital allocation perspective, we returned $3 billion to our shareholders during the quarter, comprised of 1.6 billion for our quarterly cash dividend and 1.4 billion of share repurchases with 10.8 billion remaining under our share repurchase program.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Given the confidence we have in our business and the strength of our ongoing cash flows, today we announced that we are raising our dividend by a penny to 42 cents per quarter.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: This dividend increase demonstrates our commitment to returning a minimum of 50% of free cash flow annually to our shareholders.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: To summarize, we had another quarter with top and bottom line performance exceeding our expectations driven by strong order growth and robust margins, all demonstrating the power of our innovation engine to drive strong top line growth as well as operating leverage to fuel profitability.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We remain focused on making strategic investments in innovation to capitalize on the significant growth opportunities that we see ahead.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: This will continue to be underpinned by our commitment to discipline spend management, and it's this powerful combination that continues to fuel strong cash flow and our ability to return significant value to our shareholders.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Turning to guidance, please note our Q3 and fiscal year 2026 guide assumes current tariffs and exemptions remain in place through the end of fiscal 2026.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: These assumptions remain unchanged from prior guidance.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Looking ahead, you can expect us to continue our focus on durable growth, the financial discipline driving operating leverage and continued capital returns.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: For fiscal Q3, our guidance is as follows.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We expect revenue to be in the range of 15.4 billion to 15.6 billion.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We anticipate non-gap gross margin to be in the range of 65.5% to 66.5%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Non-gap operating margin is expected to be in the range of 33.5% to 34.5%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Non-gap earnings per share is expected to range from $1.02 to $1.04.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We are assuming a non-gap effective tax rate of approximately 19%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Cisco is positioned for its strongest year ever as indicated in our guidance for fiscal year 2026, which is as follows.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We expect revenue to be in the range of 61.2 billion to 61.7 billion.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Non-gap earnings per share is expected to range from $4.13 to $4.17.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Sammy, let's now move into the Q&A.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Thank you, Mark.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Before we start the Q&A portion of the call, I'd like to remind analysts to ask one question and a single follow-up question at the same time.
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Operator, can we move to the first analyst in the queue?
speaker: SAMI BADRI - HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'm Adariani with Evercore ISI.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Your line is open.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks, Mark.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: Good afternoon.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: My two questions, maybe the first one, you folks obviously have very solid momentum on the AI side with a 5 billion AI targets of fiscal 26.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: Can you help us think about the mix between Silicon 1 versus optics in the book?
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: And then really, if you think about some of the newer products like the G300 and the P200 on the Silicon 1 side,
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: do you see these opening up incremental markets or workloads for Cisco,
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: or is it more about deepening your existing relationship?
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: I'd love to just kind of understand that side.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: And then maybe on the follow-up, Mark, you just touched on the growth margin decline in April.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: I assume it's all memory-related, but I'd love to just understand what's happening there,
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: and if you feel like that's a trough on the growth margin, memory issues or not.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: Thank you.
speaker: AMIT DARYANANI - EVERCORE ISI

text: Hey, Mick.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Thank you very much for the question.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: So on the AI infrastructure side, the 5 billion that we now have raised our estimates to during fiscal 2026 does not include any of the recently announced P200 products,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: no or G300.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Also neither of the optics solutions that we announced this week at Cisco Live.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We talked about this past quarter.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: The mix was 60% systems, 40% optics, and I think that's been reasonably consistent over the last few quarters.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Your final question about whether these open up new markets, I think these will allow us to continue to sell next-generation solutions across our existing customer base,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: and I think it'll continue to help us gain traction with the neo clouds, the sovereign clouds, et cetera, help us get the scale across technologies out and continue to sell these solutions to existing customers as well as new customers,
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: but just to reiterate, P200 and G300 are not in the 5 billion expected for fiscal 26.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Mark?
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Yeah, thanks, Emmett.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: I'll take the gross margin question.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: So really, as you look at the Q3 guide, there's two primary things at work.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: One is mix and the other is memory prices, as you mentioned.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: First off, I just want to acknowledge the significant growth in hardware that we're seeing across our existing and new platforms that have been introduced and the fact that they're accelerating much faster than previous launches.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Secondly, in terms of memory, we're going to control what we can control.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Chuck talked a little bit about the fact that we've already announced price increases.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: We're going to stay very close to that and adjust as needed going forward.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Secondly, there's some T's and C's with partners and customers that we're going to adjust to really bolster our position there.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: Thirdly, it's really leaning into our financial strength and our world-class supply chain.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: If you look at it, it's evidenced by our advanced purchase commitments that just in the last 90 days are up 1.8 billion.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: If you look at it on a year of your basis, they're up about 73%.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: A big chunk of that is around memory.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: And then lastly, I think, you know, in terms of focusing on what we can control, we're focused on financial discipline and profitability.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: And we're focused on growing EPS faster than revenue.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: You saw that in Q1.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: You saw it in Q2.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

text: And it's also in our full year FY26 guide.
speaker: MARK PATTERSON - EVP & CFO

speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: And I think that's a lot of the data that we're seeing in the manufacturing pharmaceuticals.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: So on the security update, here's what I would say.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: There's really three key points.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: Number one, we talked about the Splunk transition that has a short-term revenue headwind with the accounting treatment of on-prem versus cloud.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: The second thing is that we, you know, on the new products, and these are products that largely didn't exist three years ago.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: This is everything from secure access to XDR, to AI defense, to hyper-shield.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: We had a thousand new customers of those products during the past quarter, and it was up 100% sequentially.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: So we're seeing the ramp now where customers are adopting these new products, which is a really good sign.
speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

speaker: CHUCK ROBBINS - CHAIR & CEO

text: And as they look at the architectures that are required for a Gen. A.I., the security architecture, the network architecture, the latency requirements, all of that is leading them to look at the network architecture.
